channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Haitian migrants in Springfield, Ohio, turn to faith amid deportation fears
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ohio (AP) — At the end of his Sunday service, the pastor of the First Haitian Evangelical Church of Springfield, Ohio, asked ushers and musicians to form a circle around him as he knelt in prayer, flanked by the flags of Haiti and the United States.
item.dc:creator: LUIS ANDRES HENAO and JESSIE WARDARSKI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Intelligence sharing by the US and its allies has saved lives. Trump could test those ties
item.description: LONDON (AP) — As Russia moved closer to invading Ukraine nearly three years ago, the United States and its allies took the extraordinary step of declassifying and sharing intelligence to expose Moscow’s plans. Information flew across the Atlantic from U.S.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and DAVID KLEPPER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Top Trump administration officials in Chicago for start of immigration enforcement crackdown
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Top Trump administration officials, including “border czar” Tom Homan and the acting deputy attorney general, visited Chicago on Sunday to witness the start of ramped-up immigration enforcementin the nation’s third-largest city as federal agencies touted arrests around the country.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN and ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: A Sundance documentary called ‘The Stringer’ disputes who took AP’s 'napalm girl' photo in Vietnam
item.description: PARK CITY, Utah (AP) — After a half-century of public silence, a freelance photographer from Vietnam has asserted he took one of the most renowned and impactful photos of the 20th century — the image of a naked girl fleeing a napalm attack in South Vietnam that has long been credited to a staff phot
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colombia to send presidential plane to Honduras to pick up migrants from US flights
item.description: President Gustavo Petro has arranged for the presidential plane to facilitate the "dignified return of Colombian nationals "
item.dc:creator: Elvia Limon, NewsNation

item.title: In policy reversal, Trump eliminates help for Black and Latino communities hit harder by pollution
item.description: For four years, the Environmental Protection Agency made environmental justice one of its biggest priorities, working to improve health conditions in heavily-polluted communities often made up largely of Black, Latino and low-income Americans. Now that short-lived era is over.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL PHILLIS and ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Dolton mayor Tiffany Henyard found in contempt of court after allegedly stonewalling liquor licenses
item.description: Dolton, Illinois, Mayor Tiffany Henyard was held in contempt of court for repeatedly refusing to sign liquor licenses that had already been approved.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Freighter on the move after it was freed from ice on frozen Lake Erie
item.description: BUFFALO, N.Y. (AP) — A freighter was on its way to Canada on Sunday after it was freed from ice that trapped it in a frozen Lake Erie for days, the U.S. Coast Guard said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At Paris Fashion Week, Hermes menswear has a dash of jockey-style boldness
item.description: Hermes illuminated a gloomy Paris afternoon with its fall men’s collection Saturday, as Veronique Nichanian turned to the racetrack for inspiration.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS ADAMSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Black History Month movie celebration at AMC Theatres
item.description: The movie theater chain will bring back seven fan-favorite films from the past year for the month of February. Tickets for each show are already available to purchase online.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Private investigator first to admit guilt in offering bribes to get intoxicated drivers off the hook
item.description: A private investigator embroiled in a corruption scandal that rocked New Mexico’s law enforcement community for over a decade was the first to plead guilty to a slew of federal charges that include racketeering and bribery, according to court records released Friday.
item.dc:creator: SEJAL GOVINDARAO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Freighter remains stuck in the ice on a frozen Lake Erie
item.description: The U.S. and Canadian Coast Guards on Saturday continued efforts to break up ice and free a freighter that has been trapped in a frozen Lake Erie for days, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Oklahoma bill would make it a crime punishable by jail time to "possess" a shopping cart belonging to a business
item.description: An Oklahoma lawmaker is proposing a law that would make it a crime punishable by up to a year in jail for anyone caught “in possession of” a shopping cart taken from a store.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, Fox 5

item.title: Washington state woman is charged in the fatal shooting of a Border Patrol agent in Vermont
item.description: A Washington state woman has been charged in the fatal shooting of a U.S. Border Patrol agent in Vermont, which happened days after authorities began watching her and a German companion who also died in the roadside shootout, the FBI said Friday.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House fires some independent inspectors general
item.description: NewsNation partner The Hill reported that Trump has ousted around 17 federal watchdogs within various departments
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: WATCH: Nuns have snowball fight during winter storm
item.description: Several nuns duked it out last week, having a snowball fight during a winter storm that swept across the Deep South.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio school bus driver rescues family's pets from burning home
item.description: Bitter cold temperatures closed schools in a Stark County community on Wednesday, so it was a day off for Todd Morris as well.
item.dc:creator: Dave Nethers, KRON4

item.title: Tattoo removal expert says 1 in 7 clients asks to remove this specific tattoo
item.description: Today’s tattoo-removal experts are quite adept at ridding their clients of all types of ill-conceived tattoos. But there are a few specific categories of ill-conceived ink that they tend to treat more often than others.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Trump visits Las Vegas after whirlwind first days in office
item.description: President Trump is in Las Vegas Saturday after a whirlwind first few days in the Oval Office, filled with executive orders and travel across the country as his agenda takes form in Washington.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The only person in the world with a functioning pig organ is thriving after a record 2 months
item.description: An Alabama woman passed a major milestone Saturday to become the longest living recipient of a pig organ transplant – healthy and full of energy with her new kidney for 61 days and counting.
item.dc:creator: LAURAN NEERGAARD, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Compton Cowboys step up during Los Angeles wildfires to rescue endangered horses
item.description: The founder of the Compton Cowboys shared stories of unusual heroism and ongoing efforts to rescue horses during the devastating LA fires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: China tells Rubio to behave himself in veiled warning
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China's veteran foreign minister has issued a veiled warning to America's new secretary of state: Behave yourself.
item.dc:creator: KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Aurora borealis returns: When and where to watch the northern lights
item.description: Sky gazers in several U.S. states could get a colorful glimpse of the northern lights as we enter the weekend, thanks to a recent geomagnetic storm.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: Hegseth confirmed as Trump's defense secretary in tie-breaking vote despite turmoil over his conduct
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Pete Hegseth as the nation's defense secretary late Friday in a dramatic tie-breaking vote, swatting back questions about his qualifications to lead the Pentagon amid allegations of heavy drinking and aggressive behavior toward women.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, MARY CLARE JALONICK and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vance breaks tie to confirm Pete Hegseth as Defense secretary
item.description: Vice President Vance on Friday broke a Senate tie to confirm Pete Hegseth as President Trump's secretary of Defense, capping a bruising two-month fight over the nominee, who faced a litany of allegations that included sexual misconduct, financial mismanagement and excessive drinking. GOP Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WGN 9

item.title: Target latest company to roll back DEI programs
item.description: Target has announced it is rolling back its diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) policies, becoming the latest corporation to do so following President Donald Trump’s election.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump arrives in Los Angeles to tour wildfire damage
item.description: Prior to arriving in California Friday, Trump told reporters in North Carolina that the Federal Government would not provide aid to Los Angeles recovery efforts unless new voter ID laws were passed and changes were made to the state's water management system.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KRON4

item.title: Defense Department orders 'pause' of social media accounts
item.description: The Defense Department said it needs time to review its social media programming to match Trump's priorities.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Homeowners in Palisades Fire area angered by bank's decision
item.description: With the devastating Palisades Fire still smoldering in California, Lisa Pelton and some of her neighbors in Mandeville Canyon received an unpleasant notice from their bank: Their home equity lines of credit were being slashed.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KRON4

item.title: Gen Z is most challenging generation to work with, according to survey
item.description: (KRON) -- The younger generation has always had a tough time proving itself to older generations in the workplace. Back in the 1990s, older workers spoke disparagingly of "20-something" Gen Xers making their way into the workplace at the time.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Snowmen replace sandcastles on Alabama beach after historic winter storm
item.description: Many people who don't normally get to experience snow found unique ways to enjoy the rare sight this week as a historic winter storm moved across the South.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Convicted killer sings in front of victims' families
item.description: A man convicted of murdering two women in 1986 interrupted his own sentencing hearing on Tuesday by singing in front of his victims' families.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, WGN 9

item.title: A federal judge temporarily blocks Trump’s executive order redefining birthright citizenship
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — A federal judge on Thursday temporarily blocked President Donald Trump’s executive order denying U.S. citizenship to the children of parents living in the country illegally, calling it “blatantly unconstitutional” during the first hearing in a multi-state effort challenging the order.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON and MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration blocks federal health agencies from communicating with public
item.description: The Trump administration has put a freeze on many federal health agency communications with the public through at least the end of the month.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE and JONEL ALECCIA Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
